data.title: Trump Administration Sued by 19 U.S. States for 'Devastating' H-1B Visa Fee
data.description:
19 U.S. states, including California, New York, and Oregon, have sued the Trump administration for raising the H-1B visa fee to $100,000, saying that the price hike would have a "devastating" effect on the country. The visa allows U.S. employers to hire skilled foreign workers in roles including physicians, researchers, and nurses.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Before Trump raised the fee in September, the cost to file an H-1B visa petition typically ranged from $960 to $7,595. "By imposing this fee, the Administration is exceeding the fee-setting authority granted by Congress, which requires that fees be set based on the agency's costs, rather than arbitrarily," said the Oregon Department of Justice in a statement posted on Friday.

States Warn H-1B Fee Hike Threatens Key Public Sectors

New York Attorney General Letitia James said that the higher fee would threaten public sectors, such as healthcare and education, while harming the economy. Oregon Attorney General Dan Rayfield added that the state's education and research institutions rely on foreign workers to operate, with the higher fee creating a major challenge for employment.

Other entities have already sued the Trump administration in response to the hike, including the U.S. Chamber of Commerce and several employee unions.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) Opens 4,000th Restaurant amid Aggressive Expansion
data.description:
Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) has opened its 4,000th restaurant location, marking a significant milestone in its aggressive expansion strategy.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

CMG stock closed 4% higher on Dec. 12 amid reports of the company's newest restaurant opening in Manhattan, Kansas. The 4,000th outlet features Chipotle's signature Chipotlane drive-thru and the company's new High-Efficiency Equipment Package that improves cooking times and increases production and efficiency.

Management at Chipotle said in a news release that they are now more than halfway toward their long-term goal of operating 7,000 restaurants across the U.S. and Canada. Since CEO Scott Boatwright joined the company in 2017, Chipotle has added 1,700 new locations, representing a 70% increase in its restaurant footprint.

To commemorate the 4,000-restaurant opening, Chipotle's executive leadership team rang the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange.

Chipotle's Growth Plans

The Newport Beach, California-based company plans to open between 315 and 345 new restaurants for all of this year, with at least 80% featuring Chipotlanes. In 2026, management anticipates opening 350 to 370 new locations, including 10 to 15 international restaurants outside the U.S.

Chipotle's international presence now includes over 100 restaurants outside America, with 75 in Canada, 28 in Europe, and 11 locations in the Middle East. The company recently opened its first Chipotlane outside North America in Kuwait, and has signed agreements to expand into Mexico, South Korea, and Singapore next year.

Is CMG Stock a Buy?

The stock of Chipotle Mexican Grill has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 25 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 19 Buy and six Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average CMG price target of $45.74 implies 26.56% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why GameStop's (GME) Earnings Update Was a Loud Yawn
data.description:
Video game retailer GameStop's (GME) most recent earnings report did little to change the stock's poor performance this year. After a brief burst of optimism following a stronger-than-expected Q2, Q3 quickly brought that momentum to a halt, and shares are now down almost 30% year-to-date.

data.title: Wealthfront (WLTH) IPO Gets Tepid Response as Stock Rises 1% in Market Debut
data.description:
The initial public offering (IPO) of robo-advisor Wealthfront drew a tepid response on Wall Street with the company's stock rising only 1.36% in its market debut.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Shares of the innovative wealth management company opened trading on the Nasdaq (NDAQ) under the ticker symbol WLTH at $14 a share and ended the day at $14.19, a modest increase that showed lukewarm interest. Wealthfront priced its IPO at $14 per share and is now valued at $2.7 billion based on the closing price Dec. 12.

Wealthfront picked a bad day to go public, with all three of the major U.S. stock indices deep in the red as concerns about the health of the U.S. economy and the artificial intelligence (AI) trade continue to shake investors' confidence in the current rally.

Wealthfront's Approach to Finance

Wealthfront operates in the growing wealth management industry, which has benefited from more Americans seeking out financial advice and help managing their stock portfolios. Founded in 2008, Wealthfront caters primarily to Millennial and Gen Z investors, offering professionally managed portfolios at low costs.

The company has seen explosive growth in its cash savings accounts. It charges 0.25% annually on assets under management for its robo-advisory investment service. Traditional human advisors typically charge 1% or more for the same offering. Wealthfront almost didn't go public. In 2022, Swiss bank UBS (UBS) announced it would acquire Wealthfront for $1.4 billion in a deal that was later canceled.

Wealthfront is one of several IPOs this year that have fizzled out of the gate. High-profile market debuts from companies such as ticket reseller StubHub (STUB) and online financial services company Klarna (KLAR) have also disappointed. Both STUB and KLAR stocks are trading below their IPO prices.

Is KLAR Stock a Buy?

It's too early for any analysts to have ratings or price targets on Wealthfront's stock. So instead, we'll look at another newly public financial company, Klarna Group. The stock of Klarna has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 16 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 13 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average KLAR price target of $47.53 implies 52.83% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: DELL Stock Slips 6% after Director Egon Durban Sells $9.8M in Stock
data.description:
Shares of Dell Technologies (DELL) fell over 6% after a regulatory filing revealed that board director Egon Durban sold 71,000 shares of the company, valued at $9.8 million. The sale drew attention from investors already uneasy about high volatility in tech and AI stocks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Insider sales do not always signal trouble. Executives often sell for personal financial reasons, but the timing added pressure as Dell stock has been sensitive to shifts in sentiment about enterprise spending and AI infrastructure demand.

Meanwhile, Dell has been a standout performer so far this year, with its stock up 15%. The upside was fueled by optimism about its AI‑optimized servers and growing partnerships across the semiconductor ecosystem.

Bearish Insider Sentiment for DELL Stock

Based on informative insider transactions from the last three months, Durban is not the only insider who has sold DELL stock. Overall, TipRanks' Insider Trading Activity Tool shows that insider confidence in the stock is currently Very Negative.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: A French Court Has Temporarily Frozen €110M Worth of Google's (GOOGL) Assets
data.description:
A French court has temporarily frozen about €110 million, or $129 million, worth of tech giant Google's (GOOGL) assets in France, following a request from the administrator of Google's shutdown Russian business. This rare legal move by Russian authorities comes as tensions rise over how Europe may handle frozen Russian assets. The freeze targets shares owned by Google International and was requested by the Russian branch's court-appointed administrator. At the same time, Russia's central bank is also suing Euroclear, a Brussels-based financial institution that holds many frozen Russian assets.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The freeze in France is based on three rulings made by Moscow arbitration courts between 2024 and 2025. These courts operate under international commercial law. William Julie, the lawyer representing the Russian liquidator, explained that a Russian tribunal found Google guilty of making an illegal dividend payment in 2021 worth about $126 million. The liquidator is also trying to enforce the rulings in other countries, including Spain, Turkey, and South Africa.

Under French law, the freeze is temporary unless formal recognition proceedings begin within one month. Julie said that those documents would be filed soon and confirmed that Google France had been notified. The Paris Judicial Court will then decide whether to approve and enforce the Russian court rulings, a process that could take up to 18 months. Google can challenge the freeze during this time. If the court approves the enforcement, the frozen funds could be seized to settle the Russian judgments.

Is GOOGL Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on GOOGL stock based on 29 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average GOOGL price target of $323.64 per share implies that shares are trading near fair value.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Tumble on AI Spending Fears as Investors Rotate into Value Stocks
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower ahead of the weekend, dragged down by weakness in the tech and AI sectors.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Oracle (ORCL) stirred fears of overspending after the company raised its full-year capital expenditure guidance to $50 billion from $35 billion. In addition, reports circulated that it delayed the completion date for some of its OpenAI data centers to 2028 from 2027, although Oracle later denied the rumors. Furthermore, Broadcom (AVGO) closed with large losses after the chipmaker warned that its profit margins could fall.

"We expect Q1 consolidated gross margin to be down approximately 100 basis points sequentially, primarily reflecting a higher mix of AI revenue," said Broadcom CFO Kirsten Spears.

On Wednesday, the Fed voted 9-3 to reduce interest rates by 25 bps, although the number of dissenting voters climbed to its highest level since 2019. Kansas City Fed President Jeffrey Schmid and Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee dissented in favor of holding rates steady, while Fed Governor Stephen Miran advocated for a 50 bps reduction.

Both Schmid and Goolsbee cited the risk of sticky inflation as the main factor behind their dissents. "But I've just been uncomfortable front-loading too many rate cuts and assuming that what we've seen in inflation will be transitory," Goolsbee said on CNBC's "Squawk Box." Schmid added that modestly restrictive rates are necessary in order to combat inflation and the risk of an overheating economy. Core personal consumption expenditures (PCE), which is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation, were 2.8% in September and still above the Fed's target of 2%.

Meanwhile, the S&P 500 (SPX) received two bullish forecasts for 2026. Fundstrat's Tom Lee expects the benchmark index to rise to 7,700, powered by a new Fed Chair who could introduce an accelerated rate cut cycle. Betting markets favor President Trump nominating National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett to the position.

"After 3 years >20% yearly gains, bull market still alive," said Lee. "The significant 'Wall of Worry' is a tailwind for bull market."

Goldman Sachs predicts the SPX will reach 7,700 by the end of 2026, citing strong earnings growth powered by AI productivity gains and continued strength from Big Tech stocks. Goldman Chief U.S. equity strategist Ben Snider estimates that the S&P 500 will grow its 2026 EPS by 12% to $305.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 1.07% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 1.91%.

data.title: NVDA Stock Forecast: Analysts Say Nvidia Could Hit $258, Up 43% from Current Levels
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) stock remains in the spotlight amid growing concerns over an artificial intelligence (AI) bubble. The chipmaker's shares have already surged nearly 35% in 2025, and Wall Street sees another 43% upside over the next twelve months. Nvidia bulls continue to dominate bears, fueled by major catalysts like the AI data center boom, new GPU (graphics processing unit) launches, strong earnings momentum, and market expansion. Yet some analysts argue the shares are fairly valued given this growth potential.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

On TipRanks, NVDA stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. Also, the average Nvidia price target of $258.45 implies 42.8% upside potential from current levels. Let's understand some analysts' views in detail.

Analysts Hail Nvidia as the AI King

BofA Securities' top analyst Vivek Arya has a Buy rating and $275 price target, implying 52% upside potential. He noted that NVDA GPUs lead competitors by a full generation. Blackwell offers 10-15x better model training, while Vera Rubin is slated for the second half of 2026. Arya pointed to clear demand visibility for at least $500 billion in cumulative sales over 2025-26, with Google (GOOGL) as a key, growing customer and nearly every major model builder still relying on NVDA.

Similarly, Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers reaffirmed his $265 price target, implying 46.5% upside potential after the White House lifted the ban on export of H200 chips to China. Rakers estimates this unlocks $25 to $30 billion in annual revenue and adds $0.60 to $0.70 to earnings per share.

Nvidia Faces Rising Chip Competition

In contrast, Seaport Global analyst Jay Goldberg reiterated his Sell rating and $140 price target, implying 22.6% downside potential. He believes Nvidia faces rising competition and is using various sales tactics to cope, which are not yet fully visible in financials but are already significant and expected to grow in 2026. He noted that Alphabet (GOOGL) has done a strong job getting outside customers to use its in-house TPUs (tensor processing units). He added that although TPUs are a niche fit, they can beat Nvidia systems on several performance measures.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Wall Street rates NVDA a Strong Buy, with 43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Disney (DIS) Is Set to Land Its Second Billion-Dollar Hit of 2025
data.description:
Entertainment giant Disney (DIS) is set to officially land its second billion-dollar hit of 2025 with Zootopia 2, joining Lilo & Stitch as the only other Hollywood production to do so this year. So far, Zootopia 2 has earned $232.7 million domestically and $753.4 million overseas. "This milestone means the world to us," said Jared Bush, Chief Creative Officer at Walt Disney Animation Studios.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The only other movie to top $1 billion outside of Hollywood in 2025 is Ne Zha 2, a Chinese blockbuster that has brought in $2.2 billion since January, according to Comscore. Unsurprisingly, industry analyst Shawn Robbins said that these billion-dollar milestones have become less common in recent years. He also pointed out the impressive performance of Zootopia 2 in China, where it has already made close to $450 million. This is especially notable, as China has reduced the number of American films it allows in theaters amid rising tariffs.

In addition, Zootopia 2 set a new record in China for the biggest animated opening for a non-local film and became the highest-grossing non-local animated movie in just five days. Its success also highlights a shift in box office trends, with PG-rated family movies outperforming those rated PG-13 or R in the U.S. and Canada. In fact, PG films have grossed $2.7 billion so far in 2025, compared to PG-13's $2.5 billion and R-rated films' $2.4 billion. This trend actually began in 2024, when PG movies outsold all other ratings for the first time ever.

Is Disney Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on Disney stock based on 14 Buys, three Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average Disney price target of $137.87 per share implies 23.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
